comments.body: STATUS (handoff): OPEN, NOT claimed. Plan needs a MECHANISM REWRITE — the gate correctly refuted my API claims: (1) resolve.extract_references_from_diff extracts IMPORTS only (Python), NOT symbol definitions; (2) code-review DET detectors are Engine B grounding rules (gitleaks/opengrep patterns) selected by exec:DET routing 'detector' selectors, so a 'symbol-added-without-a-test' check (diff-parse added public defs + cross-file test correlation) does NOT fit that pattern model and needs a BESPOKE seam in the code-review pipeline; (3) code_review routing has NO 'priority' field and a new criterion id must be added to OVERLAY_IDS. RECOMMENDATION for next session: split — scope THIS child to the FEASIBLE nit down-rank (code_review/criteria_routing.json: mark docs + llm-prompts non-blocking / suppressed) with a routing test, and create a discovered_from follow-up ticket for the bespoke new-behavior-without-test detector (it is genuinely larger than the ticket implies). Alternatively design the bespoke detector seam. This child is INDEPENDENT of the stack code, so its plan-review can ground against origin/main (no REBAR_GATE_REF needed).

compiled_state.description:
# Code-review near-term nit reduction: suppress docs + llm-prompts advisory noise

## Why
Code-review advisory volume is ~5 findings/review, dominated by `docs` and `llm-prompts` nits
(0/9 high-value in the dogfooded adjudication). That noise buries the findings that matter and
trains reviewers to skim. We want less nit friction on the two lowest-value advisory surfaces
without touching the blocking posture (everything ships advisory in v1).

## Scope note — the landmine detector is split out
This story ORIGINALLY also bundled a grounding-assisted "new-behavior-without-test" detector.
Investigation established that detector needs a BESPOKE seam that does not exist yet (code-review
DET detectors are Engine-B pattern rules that scan code, not diff-parse + cross-file test
correlation; `resolve.extract_references_from_diff` is imports-only, not added-definition
extraction). Building that seam is disproportionate to this near-term nit-reduction change, so it
is SPLIT OUT to the follow-up idea `acoustic-coveting-turaco` (linked `discovered_from` this
story). This story now scopes to the feasible, self-contained down-rank half only.

## What
Introduce a `nit_suppressed` routing flag on the `docs` and `llm-prompts` code-review overlays in
`src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json`, and honour it in the deterministic Pass-3
decide (`code_review_decide`): AFTER `pass3_over_findings` returns the decided findings, the
partition loop re-buckets any finding whose `decision == "advisory"` and whose criteria are ALL
nit-suppressed into `dropped` INSTEAD of `surfaced`, overwriting its `reason` field with the
canonical string `"nit-suppressed"` (a NEW value in `code_review_decide`'s partition, alongside
the kernel `decide.py` reason vocabulary `no-verification` / `veto:cited-reference-inaccurate` /
`low-validity` / `default-advisory` — the kernel is NOT modified; the tag is applied gate-side).
The demotion is POST-pass3 (it changes only the surfaced/dropped partition, never validity/impact/
priority or the block decision). Blocking posture is untouched — the flag only affects advisory
surfacing — and a finding that ALSO maps to a non-suppressed dimension still surfaces (all-criteria
rule). Because the demotion runs before the decide op emits `surfaced`, the downstream
`code_review_coach_inputs` / `code_review_coach` steps (which consume `surfaced`) naturally receive
the reduced set — no coach change is needed.

## Scope / files
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json` — add `"nit_suppressed": true` to the `docs`
  and `llm-prompts` entries (both stay `blocking_enabled: false`).
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/registry.py` — a `nit_suppressed_criteria(routing_map=None)` helper
  returning the set of criteria flagged `nit_suppressed` (overlay-aware, mirroring
  `threshold_for` / `effective_routing`).
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/workflow_ops.py` — in `code_review_decide`, demote an advisory
  finding whose criteria are ALL nit-suppressed to `dropped`.
- `tests/unit/test_code_review_nit_routing.py` — the routing + decide-partition tests below.

No LLM-priority-threshold or blocking change here (blocking calibration is the rollout child
`raptorial-galloping-dragon`); this is advisory-surfacing only.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The packaged `src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json` marks BOTH `docs` and
      `llm-prompts` with `"nit_suppressed": true` AND keeps them `"blocking_enabled": false`; no
      other advisory overlay (e.g. `security`, `tests`, `performance`) is `nit_suppressed`. A unit
      test asserts this over the packaged routing.
- [ ] `code_review_decide` DEMOTES an advisory finding whose criteria are ALL nit-suppressed from
      `surfaced` to `dropped`, setting its `reason` to the canonical `"nit-suppressed"`; a finding
      mapping to a nit-suppressed AND a non-suppressed criterion still surfaces; a non-suppressed
      advisory finding still surfaces; and a BLOCK is never demoted. Unit-tested.
- [ ] The `nit_suppressed_criteria` registry helper reads the flag from the routing index
      (overlay-aware) and returns `{docs, llm-prompts}` for the packaged routing.
- [ ] Proving command: `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_code_review_nit_routing.py -v`.
- [ ] NO detector code ships in this story: the diff touches only `criteria_routing.json`,
      `registry.py` (the helper), `workflow_ops.py` (the partition demotion), and the test — no
      `detectors.py` change and no `new_behavior_without_test` symbol anywhere. Verifiable:
      `grep -rn new_behavior_without_test src/ tests/` returns NOTHING. (The detector itself is
      split to the `discovered_from` follow-up `acoustic-coveting-turaco`, per the Scope note.)
compiled_state.title: Code-review near-term nit reduction: suppress docs + llm-prompts advisory noise
